mailchimp.name: Mailchimp
mailchimp.deliverability_note: Mailchimp clips messages above 102 KB of HTML, hiding the rest behind a 'View entire message' link in Gmail. Aggressive image compression is required for promotional emails.
mailchimp.image_block_note: The Image content block resizes oversized images server-side but does not optimize compression — pre-compressed assets render faster and consume less subscriber bandwidth.
mailchimp.css_inlining: Mailchimp's template builder inlines styles automatically, but custom HTML imports do not. Inline before importing.
mailchimp.gif_note: GIFs autoplay in Mailchimp's preview but are stripped of animation in Outlook 2007–2019. The 'Outlook-safe' first frame must be meaningful on its own.
mailchimp.verified_domains: Mailchimp requires SPF + DKIM authentication via their hosted authentication — DMARC alignment is your responsibility.
mailchimp.spam_check: Mailchimp's Inbox Inspector requires a paid Standard plan ($20+/month). Free Mandrill/Standard users get no pre-send spam scoring.
mailchimp.subject_line_note: Mailchimp's Subject Line Helper is AI-based but limited to existing campaigns. External analysis lets you test variants before creating the campaign.

klaviyo.name: Klaviyo
klaviyo.deliverability_note: Klaviyo recommends keeping email weight under 100 KB to avoid Gmail clipping. Their built-in image CDN handles transformations but does not minimize source weight.
klaviyo.image_block_note: Klaviyo's drag-drop editor allows direct upload, but pre-compressed assets reduce render delay across slow mobile networks.
klaviyo.css_inlining: Klaviyo inlines CSS at send-time for drag-drop templates; custom HTML campaigns require manual inlining.
klaviyo.gif_note: Klaviyo's WebP support extends to animated WebP in Apple Mail and Gmail Android, but Outlook desktop still strips animation entirely.
klaviyo.verified_domains: Klaviyo requires dedicated sending domain with full SPF + DKIM + DMARC alignment for sender-score recovery.
klaviyo.spam_check: Klaviyo's Email Health Check tab provides aggregate inbox-placement data but no per-campaign pre-send rule scoring.
klaviyo.subject_line_note: Klaviyo offers AI subject line generation but does not score spam triggers, sentiment, or length penalties against benchmark data.

hubspot.name: HubSpot
hubspot.deliverability_note: HubSpot's design manager warns at >2 MB total email weight but does not auto-compress. Marketing Hub Professional includes A/B testing; Free/Starter do not.
hubspot.image_block_note: HubSpot's file manager stores uploads at original resolution. Pre-compression saves your storage quota and improves load times.
hubspot.css_inlining: HubSpot inlines CSS automatically for emails sent via Marketing Hub, but custom-coded templates and external HTML imports require manual inlining.
hubspot.gif_note: HubSpot supports GIF upload up to 2 MB but the embed wraps the image in a hyperlink that breaks animation on some Outlook variants — test in real Outlook before launch.
hubspot.verified_domains: HubSpot's connected domains feature handles SPF and DKIM. DMARC must be configured at your DNS provider; HubSpot only verifies it on send.
hubspot.spam_check: HubSpot's pre-send 'Spam content' check is rule-based and limited to subject + body word patterns. External multi-rule scoring catches what HubSpot misses.
hubspot.subject_line_note: HubSpot's content assistant rewrites subject lines via AI but does not provide a quantified spam-trigger score or sentiment analysis.

brevo.name: Brevo
brevo.deliverability_note: Brevo (formerly Sendinblue) maintains a free tier with 300 emails/day. Their internal deliverability score is calculated post-send; pre-send hygiene is your responsibility.
brevo.image_block_note: Brevo's drag-drop editor compresses on upload (lossy ~80% quality JPEG) — pre-compression with explicit quality settings produces more predictable results.
brevo.css_inlining: Brevo inlines CSS automatically for drag-drop and template builder emails; raw HTML imports do not.
brevo.gif_note: Brevo's GIF support is universal but the platform recommends MP4 video over GIF for newer campaigns. GIFs over 1 MB trigger warnings in their design manager.
brevo.verified_domains: Brevo requires DNS verification (SPF + DKIM) before sending. DMARC monitoring is offered in their paid tiers only.
brevo.spam_check: Brevo's pre-send check is binary (likely spam / probably fine) without exposing the specific triggers. Detailed rule-based scoring catches deliverability issues earlier.
brevo.subject_line_note: Brevo offers basic subject line preview across clients but no spam-trigger lexicon, length scoring, or sentiment analysis.

mailerlite.name: MailerLite
mailerlite.deliverability_note: MailerLite's free plan supports up to 12,000 emails/month with HTML campaigns. Their drag-drop editor enforces 600px max width by default.
mailerlite.image_block_note: MailerLite compresses uploads to JPEG quality 75 automatically. Pre-compressed WebP imports keep your originals untouched.
mailerlite.css_inlining: MailerLite inlines CSS automatically; HTML imports require pre-inlining to ensure Outlook compatibility.
mailerlite.gif_note: MailerLite supports GIF upload but their previewer shows static first frame only. Test animation behavior in real clients before sending.
mailerlite.verified_domains: MailerLite requires SPF + DKIM authentication. DMARC policy is your responsibility; their docs reference policy=none as the default.
mailerlite.spam_check: MailerLite includes a basic spam-trigger checker on paid plans only. Free tier has no pre-send scoring.
mailerlite.subject_line_note: MailerLite's subject line tester is preview-only; no quantitative spam-trigger or sentiment scoring.

activecampaign.name: ActiveCampaign
activecampaign.deliverability_note: ActiveCampaign's deliverability score is opaque — they show category (Excellent/Good/Fair) without exposing the contributing factors. Pre-send rule analysis fills that gap.
activecampaign.image_block_note: ActiveCampaign's media library accepts up to 5 MB per image but does not optimize for email render. Sub-1MB pre-compressed images keep total payload under Gmail's clipping threshold.
activecampaign.css_inlining: ActiveCampaign auto-inlines drag-drop email styles. Custom HTML campaigns require manual inlining for Outlook compatibility.
activecampaign.gif_note: ActiveCampaign supports animated GIFs but the platform warns about size impact on deliverability for large GIFs (>500 KB).
activecampaign.verified_domains: ActiveCampaign supports SPF, DKIM, and DMARC alignment via custom subdomain (mail.yourdomain.com). DMARC policy configuration is yours.
activecampaign.spam_check: ActiveCampaign's spam-test feature requires the Plus plan ($49/mo). External free testing covers pre-send hygiene without the per-test cost.
activecampaign.subject_line_note: ActiveCampaign's subject line analyzer is AI-based but limited to the campaign editor. External multi-factor scoring works across all your campaign drafts.

convertkit.name: Kit (ConvertKit)
convertkit.deliverability_note: Kit (formerly ConvertKit) ships with their own deliverability monitoring. Their visual editor enforces a clean text-heavy aesthetic that performs well in Gmail's primary tab.
convertkit.image_block_note: Kit's image uploader compresses to JPEG quality 80. Pre-compressed PNG/WebP imports preserve creator brand integrity.
convertkit.css_inlining: Kit's visual editor inlines CSS automatically. Custom HTML emails (advanced plans only) require manual inlining.
convertkit.gif_note: Kit supports animated GIFs in broadcast campaigns. The platform recommends sub-1 MB GIFs to keep total email weight under deliverability thresholds.
convertkit.verified_domains: Kit handles SPF + DKIM authentication via their sending domain. Custom DKIM signatures require their Creator Pro plan.
convertkit.spam_check: Kit's pre-send check is rule-based and surfaces high-risk content. Detailed rule-by-rule scoring identifies precisely which triggers fire.
convertkit.subject_line_note: Kit's subject line tester previews across clients but does not score spam triggers or sentiment quantitatively.

constantcontact.name: Constant Contact
constantcontact.deliverability_note: Constant Contact's deliverability dashboard is post-send only. Pre-send hygiene checks catch issues before they affect your sender reputation.
constantcontact.image_block_note: Constant Contact's image library has a 5 MB per-file limit but no automatic compression. Pre-compressed assets cut total email weight significantly.
constantcontact.css_inlining: Constant Contact's templates inline CSS automatically. Custom HTML upload (advanced plans only) requires manual inlining.
constantcontact.gif_note: Constant Contact supports animated GIFs in their builder. Files above 1 MB trigger render warnings in their preview.
constantcontact.verified_domains: Constant Contact handles SPF + DKIM. DMARC alignment requires the Pro plan and custom DNS configuration.
constantcontact.spam_check: Constant Contact's pre-send check is rule-based and shows a binary likely-spam flag without per-rule breakdown.
constantcontact.subject_line_note: Constant Contact's subject line tester previews across clients but does not score spam triggers or sentiment quantitatively.

campaignmonitor.name: Campaign Monitor
campaignmonitor.deliverability_note: Campaign Monitor's design language emphasizes typography over imagery. Pre-compressed images preserve their brand-forward aesthetic.
campaignmonitor.image_block_note: Campaign Monitor's drag-drop editor accepts uploads but does not optimize for email render. Pre-compression reduces load delay across mobile clients.
campaignmonitor.css_inlining: Campaign Monitor's templates inline CSS automatically. Custom HTML imports require manual inlining for Outlook compatibility.
campaignmonitor.gif_note: Campaign Monitor supports animated GIFs but recommends keeping animation duration under 5 seconds for engagement.
campaignmonitor.verified_domains: Campaign Monitor offers SPF and DKIM with custom sending domain. DMARC policy is configured at your DNS provider.
campaignmonitor.spam_check: Campaign Monitor's deliverability dashboard is post-send. External pre-send scoring catches issues before they hit subscriber inboxes.
campaignmonitor.subject_line_note: Campaign Monitor offers AI subject line generation but does not quantify spam triggers or sentiment.

sendgrid.name: SendGrid
sendgrid.deliverability_note: SendGrid's Marketing Campaigns and Transactional API serve different purposes. Pre-send rule analysis is essential for transactional flows where you control the HTML directly.
sendgrid.image_block_note: SendGrid's image library has a 6 MB per-file limit. Pre-compressed assets reduce per-message bandwidth, which compounds at scale.
sendgrid.css_inlining: SendGrid's Marketing Campaigns inline CSS automatically. Transactional emails sent via API require pre-inlining.
sendgrid.gif_note: SendGrid supports animated GIFs. Their Marketing Campaigns editor warns at >2 MB total email weight but does not block sending.
sendgrid.verified_domains: SendGrid requires domain authentication (SPF + DKIM) via subdomain CNAME records. DMARC alignment is your DNS configuration.
sendgrid.spam_check: SendGrid's inbox-placement testing requires their Email Validation product (paid). External pre-send rule scoring is free and immediate.
sendgrid.subject_line_note: SendGrid offers subject line A/B testing on Marketing Campaigns but no quantitative spam-trigger scoring.

postmark.name: Postmark
postmark.deliverability_note: Postmark is transactional-only. Pre-send rule analysis matters because transactional emails are time-critical and a deliverability issue stops user flows immediately.
postmark.image_block_note: Postmark does not host images — you embed external URLs. Pre-compressed images at your CDN reduce render time across all clients.
postmark.css_inlining: Postmark requires you to inline CSS yourself. Their MailMason templates use MJML which compiles to inlined HTML at build time.
postmark.gif_note: Postmark supports GIFs via external image references. Animation duration and weight are your responsibility.
postmark.verified_domains: Postmark requires SPF and DKIM via DNS verification. They strongly recommend DMARC with p=quarantine or stricter.
postmark.spam_check: Postmark's spam-scoring is post-send via SpamAssassin. Pre-send rule scoring catches issues before the message ever hits their servers.
postmark.subject_line_note: Postmark does not offer subject line analysis. External tools fill this gap for transactional templates.

mailgun.name: Mailgun
mailgun.deliverability_note: Mailgun is API-first transactional + marketing. Pre-send hygiene scoring is essential since you control the HTML directly.
mailgun.image_block_note: Mailgun does not host images. Pre-compressed assets at your CDN reduce per-message overhead.
mailgun.css_inlining: Mailgun requires you to inline CSS yourself. Their templating engine does not auto-inline.
mailgun.gif_note: Mailgun supports GIFs via external image references. Weight and animation behavior are your responsibility.
mailgun.verified_domains: Mailgun requires SPF and DKIM via DNS. DMARC monitoring is available on their Foundation plan and higher.
mailgun.spam_check: Mailgun does not offer pre-send rule scoring. External free tools fill this gap.
mailgun.subject_line_note: Mailgun does not offer subject line analysis. External tools provide it.

ses.name: AWS SES
ses.deliverability_note: Amazon SES is the lowest-cost transactional sender ($0.10 per 1,000 emails) but provides no built-in deliverability tooling. Pre-send rule analysis is essential.
ses.image_block_note: SES does not host images. Pre-compressed assets at your CDN are critical at SES's typical high-volume sends.
ses.css_inlining: SES sends raw MIME — you handle CSS inlining yourself. Their template feature does not auto-inline.
ses.gif_note: SES supports GIFs via external image references. SES has no built-in size warnings.
ses.verified_domains: SES requires identity verification (domain or email). SPF, DKIM, DMARC are your DNS configuration.
ses.spam_check: SES has no spam-scoring layer. External pre-send rule analysis fills the gap.
ses.subject_line_note: SES has no subject line analysis. External tools provide it.

drip.name: Drip
drip.deliverability_note: Drip focuses on e-commerce marketing automation. Their pre-send checks are limited to broken-link detection; pre-send rule scoring catches deliverability triggers their tool misses.
drip.image_block_note: Drip's media library compresses uploads to JPEG quality 80. Pre-compressed WebP imports preserve color fidelity for product imagery.
drip.css_inlining: Drip inlines CSS automatically in their visual editor. Custom HTML imports require manual inlining.
drip.gif_note: Drip supports animated GIFs in product galleries and animated CTAs. They recommend sub-1 MB for inbox-friendly weight.
drip.verified_domains: Drip requires SPF and DKIM via DNS. DMARC policy is your DNS configuration.
drip.spam_check: Drip's pre-send check is binary. External rule-by-rule scoring identifies specific triggers.
drip.subject_line_note: Drip offers A/B testing for subject lines but no quantitative spam-trigger scoring.

getresponse.name: GetResponse
getresponse.deliverability_note: GetResponse offers built-in deliverability score but only on their Marketing Automation plan ($59+/mo). Free pre-send rule analysis catches issues without the subscription cost.
getresponse.image_block_note: GetResponse's drag-drop editor compresses uploads to JPEG quality 80. Pre-compressed PNG imports preserve transparent regions.
getresponse.css_inlining: GetResponse inlines CSS automatically in their template builder. Custom HTML imports require manual inlining.
getresponse.gif_note: GetResponse supports GIFs in their drag-drop and HTML editors. Their preview shows the first frame only.
getresponse.verified_domains: GetResponse requires SPF and DKIM via DNS verification. DMARC alignment is your DNS configuration.
getresponse.spam_check: GetResponse's spam-trigger check is rule-based but limited to subject and body word patterns.
getresponse.subject_line_note: GetResponse offers AI subject line generation. External quantitative scoring catches multi-factor risks their AI misses.
